title: Control Stack FAIL: seismology trace claims final_orbit_family=satellite, breaking cross-domain equivalence
_fixture_purpose: FAIL fixture: cymatics trace is correct (singularity→satellite→cosmos, k=2), but the seismology trace claims orbit_trajectory=[singularity,satellite,satellite] and final_orbit_family=satellite instead of cosmos. The cross_domain_claim nonetheless declares orbit_trajectory_preserved=true and compiler_law_domain_generic=true — both lies. Validator recomputes cross-domain consistency (CS3) and detects the mismatch, then catches the inconsistent cross_domain_claim (CS7, CS9). Fail types: ORBIT_TRAJECTORY_MISMATCH, CROSS_DOMAIN_CLAIM_INCONSISTENT, STACK_INCONSISTENCY.

domain_traces.notes: INCORRECTLY declared: claims final orbit is satellite, not cosmos. surface_wave maps to cosmos, not satellite — this claim misrepresents the QA orbit structure of the seismic domain.

cross_domain_claim.notes: INCORRECT claim: orbit_trajectory_preserved=true despite traces disagreeing on final orbit. cymatics ends in cosmos, seismology ends in satellite — the trajectories are NOT equal. The cross_domain_claim is inconsistent with the trace data.

validation_checks.description: domain_instance_refs[0] is a known domain_instance schema
domain_instance_refs[1] is a known domain_instance schema

validation_checks.details: compiler_law_domain_generic=true but orbit trajectory is NOT preserved — STACK_INCONSISTENCY

validation_checks.description: canonical_orbit_trajectory matches all domain trace orbit_trajectories
canonical_path_length_k matches all domain trace path_length_k values

fail_ledger.message: domain_traces disagree on orbit_trajectory and final_orbit_family: cymatics ends cosmos, seismology ends satellite. Cross-domain equivalence requires identical orbit trajectories across all domain instances.
cross_domain_claim.orbit_trajectory_preserved=true but traces show different trajectories. The claim contradicts the trace data.
cross_domain_claim.compiler_law_domain_generic=true but orbit trajectory is not preserved across domains. Domain-genericity requires orbit invariance; this cert does not establish it.
